tldr: Congressional trading remains heavily concentrated in mega-cap technology and AI-adjacent names, with massive cluster buying across AAPL, NVDA, and MSFT totaling over $49 million in notional volume. Beyond the broad tech accumulation, notable individual moves include a massive $5M+ buy of AESI by Rep. Chip Roy, significant new positioning in INTC and UBER by Rep. Nancy Pelosi, and targeted medical device selling by Sen. Tina Smith's spouse.

highlights.headline: Mega-Cap Tech & AI Conviction
highlights.detail: The cohort is aggressively accumulating mega-cap tech, led by AAPL ($32.7M across 27 members), NVDA ($10.6M across 17 members), and MSFT ($5.9M across 30 members). Secondary AI and semiconductor plays like TSM, AVGO, and PLTR also saw strong net buying.

highlights.headline: Chip Roy's Massive AESI Bet
highlights.detail: Rep. Chip Roy executed an unusually large purchase of AESI valued between $5.0M and $25.0M on April 30, followed by a minor $100K-$250K trim two weeks later.

highlights.headline: Pelosi Buys Intel and Uber
highlights.detail: Rep. Nancy Pelosi disclosed a $1.0M–$5.0M purchase of Intel (INTC) and a $500K–$1.0M purchase of Uber (UBER) on May 29, signaling fresh capital deployment into the legacy chipmaker and the ride-hailing giant.

highlights.headline: Jacobs Exits Qualcomm
highlights.detail: Rep. Sara Jacobs executed two large sales of Qualcomm (QCOM) on consecutive days in early May, offloading between $1.0M and $2.0M in total notional value.

highlights.headline: Medical Device Trims by Smith
highlights.detail: Sen. Tina Smith's spouse consistently sold off medical device makers Insulet (PODD) and DexCom (DXCM) across late April and early May, executing four separate trades in the $100K-$250K bracket.

highlights.headline: Gottheimer's MSFT Rebalancing
highlights.detail: Rep. Josh Gottheimer executed simultaneous buys and sells of Microsoft (MSFT) in the $250K–$1.0M brackets on May 19, indicative of portfolio rebalancing or options-related assignments rather than directional conviction.
